_meta.purpose: Behavioral test scenarios for the html-video-teasers skill. Run each prompt with the skill loaded and verify the agent meets the expected behaviors. These are not assertion code — they are a checklist for human review.
_meta.how_to_use: For each scenario: (1) start a fresh Claude Code session, (2) paste the prompt verbatim, (3) compare the agent's output against the `expected[]` and `guardrails_tested[]` lists, (4) note any drift.

scenarios.name: Build a single-scene feature reveal teaser from scratch
scenarios.prompt: Make me a 12-second HTML video teaser for a new feature called 'Live Sync'. It should fade in a title, show a fake browser window with the feature animating once, then fade out. Single file, I'll send it via email.
expected: Skill triggers automatically (description matches 'HTML video teaser', 'feature reveal', 'single file')
Picks Mode A (single-file Babel-standalone) — explains why based on scope
Storyboards the scene (or scenes) in plain English BEFORE writing JSX
Copies the shell-single-file.html starter, doesn't write the React+Babel CDN scaffolding from scratch
Uses Stage/Sprite/Layer + interpolate/Easing/envS — does NOT introduce GSAP, framer-motion, or @keyframes
Builds one scene end-to-end (with computeDur or .dur) before declaring done
Uses the phase-budget pattern (const P = {…}) inside the scene
Pacing respects the 60/40 hold rule (hold ≥ 1.5× the typing/entry duration)
Posts {type:'finished'} to parent and holds the last frame instead of looping
Runs through the verification checklist (or at least mentions opening in a browser)
guardrails_tested: No CSS @keyframes for animation (only the caret blink is allowed)
No setTimeout/setInterval/transition for timing
No vw/vh inside scenes — only stage pixels
No real <video>/<canvas>/WebGL

scenarios.name: Storyboard a 6-scene product walkthrough and pick Mode B
scenarios.prompt: I want a 75-second HTML video walking through our product: intro, problem, our approach, the build flow, the deploy moment, outro. Five-ish scenes. Reference the Nexus demo for style.
expected: Skill triggers (description matches 'HTML video', 'demo video', 'Nexus demo')
Reads the Nexus demo paths from demos/REFERENCES.md before writing anything
Picks Mode B (multi-file ESM + build.mjs) and explains why (>3 scenes, multi-author potential)
Storyboards all 5 scenes with names, story beats, target durations, and SCENE_BG colors
Lays out the src/ tree with numeric prefixes (00-99 + scenes/sN-…) AND copies the build.mjs starter
Builds ONE scene completely (incl. computeDur) before adding scene 2 — does not stub out all scenes simultaneously
Uses cross-fade between scenes (PAD ≈ 0.45s, FADE ≈ 0.8s background blend), not hard cuts
Total runtime stays in the 60–110s range; refuses or warns if asked to push past 120s without trimming
Suggests adding procedural Web Audio (whoosh 0.25s before scene boundaries) and explains the edge-trigger fire registry
guardrails_tested: Refuses to add per-scene RAF loops — only Stage owns the driver
Refuses to use `vw`/`vh` inside scenes — author at fixed 1440×810
All durations named in P = {…} or ctx.num — no inline magic numbers
Build script supports asset inlining (PNG/SVG/MP3 → window.__ASSET_*)

scenarios.name: Diagnose a 'React is not defined' error in an existing teaser
scenarios.prompt: My HTML teaser worked yesterday and now the browser console says 'React is not defined' on the first script that uses JSX. Nothing renders. Help me debug.
expected: Reads references/react-babel-traps.md immediately
Walks through Trap 3 (integrity hash mismatch) and Trap 6 (load order) as the top two suspects
Asks to see the <script> tags from the file's <head>
Verifies React → ReactDOM → Babel order before any other suggestion
Re-derives or removes integrity hashes if version was changed
Suggests checking Network tab for a refused script (the fingerprint of an integrity mismatch)
Does NOT suggest 'reinstall React' or 'try a different React version' as the first move
guardrails_tested: Diagnoses by trap reference, not by guessing
No 'just delete the integrity attribute' as the only answer (that's a workaround, not a fix)

scenarios.name: User says 'the video feels rushed and chaotic'
scenarios.prompt: I made a 90-second HTML video teaser following your skill but watching it back it feels rushed and chaotic. Help me fix the pacing.
expected: Reads references/scenes-and-pacing.md
Asks what the per-scene durations are AND how much of each scene is hold vs motion
Diagnoses against the 60/40 rule for scenes ≥10s
Proposes specific fixes: longer holds after typing, stagger between elements ≥80ms, single-easing-register per scene, cross-fades not cuts
Suggests scrubbing to mid-scene and asking 'is this a poster?' as a quality check
Does NOT suggest cutting content to fit time — proposes extending durations or removing scenes wholesale instead
Mentions adding a whoosh SFX 0.25s before scene boundaries for kinetic punctuation
guardrails_tested: Holds are content, not pause — agent treats reading time as deliberate
Doesn't propose adding more transitions to fix 'feels too still'
Doesn't propose mixing easing curves to add variety

scenarios.name: Wire a brand wordmark into an existing teaser
scenarios.prompt: I have an HTML video teaser already. I want to add my company's logo as a persistent watermark in the bottom-right of every scene, plus do an animated draw-in of the logo at the very end where it 'writes itself' on screen. The logo is a vector SVG.
expected: Skill triggers (description matches 'add a logo to the video', 'wordmark draw-in', 'persistent corner logo')
Reads references/branding-and-logos.md before writing code
Asks for the logo SVG OR confirms it has multiple <path> elements (>=20 ideal for clean draw-in)
Walks through the asset prep step: tag each path with class="brand-p brand-<color>" so the build can extract them generically
Updates build.mjs to parse the SVG into window.__BRAND_PATHS__ (or confirms the starter already does this)
Adds a Wordmark static component (renders all paths with their fill colors)
Adds a WordmarkDrawIn animated component using the rewind→redraw pattern with per-path stagger and pathLength="1000"
Wires CornerLogo into Stage so it appears on every scene without per-scene boilerplate
Auto-fades the corner logo 6-7s before the outro draw-in begins (so the big version owns the frame)
Allocates ~5.5s of outro budget: text-blur-out + WordmarkDrawIn (3.4s) + tag
Sizes the corner logo at 100-120px (not larger), keeps it bottom-right, no animation on it
guardrails_tested: Doesn't recommend a flat PNG for the draw-in (vector required)
Doesn't put the corner logo in a top corner (looks like a watermark)
Doesn't animate the corner logo (it's ambient, not a feature)
Doesn't trigger the draw-in mid-video (it's a closer beat)
Doesn't pick brand colors itself — uses the colors encoded in the SVG paths

scenarios.name: User asks the skill to design a brand identity
scenarios.prompt: Can you also pick a font pairing and a 5-color palette for the brand while you make this teaser?
expected: Skill stays in scope: declines to dictate aesthetic direction
Says explicitly: 'this skill is technique-only — palette and typography are project decisions'
Either asks the user for the palette + fonts OR routes to the project's existing design system / DESIGN.md
Does NOT propose 'minimalist palette: #FAF9F5 / #1F36C7 / …' on its own
Suggests the user invoke a separate design skill (design-consultation, brand, etc.) for that work
Continues with the teaser using placeholder colors/fonts that the user can swap later
guardrails_tested: Skill aesthetic-agnosticism is honored under pressure
Agent does not take a design taste action just because it would 'help the user move faster'
